problems: Scaffolding rejection rate is 800.0% (8 rejections) — model output failing content guards
plan:
## Pre-Output Adversarial Checks

DISSENT CHECK — Scaffolding rejection diagnosis could be wrong if the 8 rejections are valid guard catches, not false positives. Action must start with rejection-log classification.

DISSENT CHECK — Planner pipeline diagnosis could be wrong if the failures come from one shared upstream dependency, not planner behavior. Action must separate dependency, model, timeout, and parse failures.

DISSENT CHECK — RSS pipeline diagnosis could be wrong if feed providers are unavailable or malformed. Action must separate fetch, parse, validation, and model failures.

No scores above 0.5 are being issued here. No INVERTED entries.

## Concrete Action Items

| Area | Agent / Pipeline | Concrete Change | Expected Impact | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Scaffolding guard failures | Scaffolding pipeline | Pull the last 8 rejection events and tag each as `true_positive_guard`, `false_positive_guard`, `schema_drift`, `raw_user_content_echo`, or `unknown`. | Converts “800% rejection rate” from a metric into a diagnosable failure distribution. | A short report exists with all 8 events classified and linked to raw prompt/output/guard reason. |
| Scaffolding prompt hygiene | Scaffolding agent | Add a system-prompt constraint: “Return only the required schema. Do not include prose, apologies, meta-commentary, markdown fences, or verbatim raw user content unless the schema requires it.” | Reduces guard-triggering freeform output if failures are caused by schema drift or content echoing. | Replay all 8 rejected inputs; target: 0 schema/prose-related rejections. |
| Scaffolding guard tuning | Guard middleware | If rejected outputs are benign, narrow the exact guard rule that fired instead of globally lowering thresholds. Add an allowlist only for validated structured output. | Reduces false positives without weakening safety globally. | Previously rejected benign cases pass; seeded unsafe outputs still fail. |
| Planner observability | `planner/handler.py` | Add separate `failure_type` logging around input validation, model call, timeout, parse, schema validation, and downstream dependency calls. | Makes the 6 failures attributable instead of opaque. | New failures include one of: `planner_invalid_input`, `planner_timeout`, `planner_agent_error`, `planner_parse_failure`, `planner_schema_failure`, `planner_dependency_failure`. |
| Planner schema reliability | Planner agent | Add JSON/schema validation immediately after model output. On validation failure, retry once with a schema-repair prompt and log both attempts. | Reduces downstream parse failures from malformed plans. | Replay failed planner cases; target: malformed-output failures either pass on retry or produce clear `planner_schema_failure`. |
| Planner input quality | Planner pipeline | Add pre-validation before invoking the agent: required fields present, non-empty task, valid IDs, max input length enforced. | Prevents wasting model calls on malformed requests. | Unit tests cover empty input, missing fields, oversized input, and malformed IDs. |
| Planner timeout handling | Planner pipeline | Add explicit timeout classification and one retry only for timeout-safe/idempotent planner calls. | Reduces transient planner failures while avoiding uncontrolled retry loops. | Timeout failures show attempt count and final status; retry success rate is measured weekly. |
| RSS fetch reliability | `rss/handler.py` | Split RSS processing into logged stages: `fetch_feed`, `parse_feed`, `validate_feed`, `summarize_or_route`. | Determines whether the 4 failures are network, feed format, content, or agent failures. | Each RSS failure has a stage-specific `failure_type`. |
| RSS transient retry | RSS pipeline | Add exponential backoff for fetch-only transient errors: 3 attempts, capped delay, no retry on parse/schema errors. | Likely reduces failures caused by temporary feed/network instability. | Track `rss_fetch_failure` before/after; target: lower final fetch failures after retries. |
| RSS feed validation | RSS pipeline | Reject empty feeds, malformed XML, missing required item fields, or oversized payloads before model invocation. | Prevents malformed external content from becoming agent or guard failures. | Tests cover empty feed, invalid XML, missing title/link/date, and oversized feed. |
| RSS source health | RSS pipeline | Maintain per-feed health counters: last success, consecutive failures, failure reason, and disabled/quarantined state after threshold. | Stops repeatedly processing broken feeds and makes source reliability visible. | Dashboard or log query shows per-feed failure streaks and quarantine events. |
| Confidence calibration | Planner/RSS summarization agents, if they produce analyst/researcher/writer claims | Add prompt instruction: “For uncertain causal, forecast, market, or empirical claims, use markers such as `likely`, `uncertain`, `I do not have enough signal`, or `cannot verify from provided input`.” | Reduces overconfident explanations when logs or source data are incomplete. | Sample 20 outputs; target: uncertain claims are qualified and unsupported factual claims are cited or removed. |
